GeSTAR

Germanium – Science Technology Advancement Research

A collaborative research initiative dedicated to advancing germanium crystal growth and detector technology. Our mission is to enable cutting-edge science for dark matter, neutrino experiments, and global discovery.

Mission

  • Advance germanium detector technologies for dark matter and neutrino experiments.
  • Support physics education and early-career training.
  • Build global collaboration around germanium science.

What is Ge-STAR?

Ge-STAR (Germanium – Science Technology Advancement Research) is a collaborative research initiative focused on growing high-purity germanium crystals and developing next-generation radiation detectors. Our work contributes to fundamental research in particle physics, space science, and homeland security.

Scientific Goals

  • Detect dark matter and neutrinos using ultra-low-background HPGe detectors
  • Study neutrinoless double beta decay with high resolution
  • Push sensitivity boundaries of rare-event detection
Explore Science »

Interested in Collaborating?

We welcome institutions, researchers, and students to join our growing global network. Explore our facilities and partnership opportunities.
